optimize_0plus_b_sound.solution:
Fixpoint optimize_0plus_b (b : bexp) : bexp :=
  match b with
  | BTrue => BTrue
  | BFalse => BFalse
  | BEq a1 a2 => BEq (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BNeq a1 a2 => BNeq (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BLe a1 a2 => BLe (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BGt a1 a2 => BGt (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BNot b1 => BNot (optimize_0plus_b b1)
  | BAnd b1 b2 => BAnd (optimize_0plus_b b1) (optimize_0plus_b b2)
  end.

Theorem optimize_0plus_b_sound : forall b,
  beval (optimize_0plus_b b) = beval b.
Proof.
  induction b; simpl;
    try (rewrite optimize_0plus_sound; rewrite optimize_0plus_sound);
    try reflexivity;
    try (rewrite IHb; reflexivity);
    try (rewrite IHb1; rewrite IHb2; reflexivity).
Qed.
optimize_0plus_b_sound.explanation: Apply optimize_0plus to all arithmetic sub-expressions in a boolean expression. Soundness follows from optimize_0plus_sound.

optimize.solution:
(* Example: optimize away multiplication by 1 *)
Fixpoint optimize_mult1 (a : aexp) : aexp :=
  match a with
  | ANum n => ANum n
  | AId x => AId x
  | APlus a1 a2 => APlus (optimize_mult1 a1) (optimize_mult1 a2)
  | AMinus a1 a2 => AMinus (optimize_mult1 a1) (optimize_mult1 a2)
  | AMult (ANum 1) a2 => optimize_mult1 a2
  | AMult a1 (ANum 1) => optimize_mult1 a1
  | AMult a1 a2 => AMult (optimize_mult1 a1) (optimize_mult1 a2)
  end.

Theorem optimize_mult1_sound : forall a st,
  aeval st (optimize_mult1 a) = aeval st a.
Proof.
  induction a; simpl; try reflexivity; try (rewrite IHa1; rewrite IHa2; reflexivity).
  - (* AMult *) destruct a1; try (simpl; rewrite IHa2; destruct n; try reflexivity; simpl;
      destruct n; try (simpl; rewrite Nat.add_0_r; reflexivity); simpl; rewrite IHa2; reflexivity);
    try (simpl; rewrite IHa1; rewrite IHa2; reflexivity).
    destruct a2; try (simpl; rewrite IHa1; reflexivity);
    try (simpl; rewrite IHa1; reflexivity).
Qed.
optimize.explanation: Design exercise: one possible optimization eliminates multiplication by 1. The proof proceeds by structural induction on the arithmetic expression, with case analysis on AMult.

beval_rules.explanation: Informal exercise: write inference rules mirroring the beval function for BTrue, BFalse, BEq, BNeq, BLe, BGt, BNot, BAnd.

bevalR.explanation: Define bevalR as an inductive relation mirroring the beval function, then prove equivalence by induction in both directions.

ceval_example2.solution:
Example ceval_example2:
  empty_st =[
    X := 0;
    Y := 1;
    Z := 2
  ]=> (Z !-> 2 ; Y !-> 1 ; X !-> 0).
Proof.
  apply E_Seq with (X !-> 0).
  - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 1 ; X !-> 0).
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
Qed.
ceval_example2.explanation: Chain three assignments using E_Seq with appropriate intermediate states.

pup_to_n.solution:
Definition pup_to_n : com :=
  <{ Y := 0 ;
     while X <> 0 do
       Y := Y + X ;
       X := X - 1
     end }>.

Theorem pup_to_2_ceval :
  (X !-> 2) =[
    pup_to_n
  ]=> (X !-> 0 ; Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
Proof.
  unfold pup_to_n.
  apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
  - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - apply E_WhileTrue with (X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
    + reflexivity.
    + apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
      * apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
      * apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    + apply E_WhileTrue with (X !-> 0 ; Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
      * reflexivity.
      * apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
        -- apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
        -- apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
      * apply E_WhileFalse. reflexivity.
Qed.
pup_to_n.explanation: The program sums 1 to X by accumulating into Y while decrementing X. The proof traces each iteration.

loop_never_stops.solution:
Theorem loop_never_stops : forall st st',
  ~(st =[ loop ]=> st').
Proof.
  intros st st' contra. unfold loop in contra.
  remember <{ while true do skip end }> as loopdef eqn:Heqloopdef.
  induction contra; inversion Heqloopdef; subst.
  - discriminate.
  - apply IHcontra2. reflexivity.
Qed.
loop_never_stops.explanation: By induction on the evaluation derivation, E_WhileFalse gives a contradiction (true <> false), and E_WhileTrue applies the IH.

no_whiles_eqv.explanation: Define an inductive proposition mirroring the boolean function, then prove equivalence using andb_true_iff.

no_whiles_terminating.solution:
Theorem no_whiles_terminating : forall c st,
  no_whilesR c -> exists st', st =[ c ]=> st'.
Proof.
  intros c st H. generalize dependent st.
  induction H; intros st.
  - exists st. apply E_Skip.
  - exists (x !-> aeval st a ; st). apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - destruct (IHno_whilesR1 st) as [st' H1].
    destruct (IHno_whilesR2 st') as [st'' H2].
    exists st''. apply E_Seq with st'; assumption.
  - destruct (beval st b) eqn:Hb.
    + destruct (IHno_whilesR1 st) as [st' H1].
      exists st'. apply E_IfTrue; assumption.
    + destruct (IHno_whilesR2 st) as [st' H2].
      exists st'. apply E_IfFalse; assumption.
Qed.
no_whiles_terminating.explanation: By induction on no_whilesR, each case produces an evaluation derivation. Skip and assignment are direct; sequence and if use IH.

stack_compiler.solution:
Fixpoint s_execute (st : state) (stack : list nat)
                   (prog : list sinstr)
                 : list nat :=
  match prog with
  | [] => stack
  | i :: prog' =>
    let newstack :=
      match i with
      | SPush n => n :: stack
      | SLoad x => st x :: stack
      | SPlus => match stack with
                 | n1 :: n2 :: rest => (n2 + n1) :: rest
                 | _ => stack
                 end
      | SMinus => match stack with
                  | n1 :: n2 :: rest => (n2 - n1) :: rest
                  | _ => stack
                  end
      | SMult => match stack with
                 | n1 :: n2 :: rest => (n2 * n1) :: rest
                 | _ => stack
                 end
      end
    in s_execute st newstack prog'
  end.

Example s_execute1 :
     s_execute empty_st []
       [SPush 5; SPush 3; SPush 1; SMinus]
   = [2; 5].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example s_execute2 :
     s_execute (X !-> 3) [3;4]
       [SPush 4; SLoad X; SMult; SPlus]
   = [15; 4].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Fixpoint s_compile (e : aexp) : list sinstr :=
  match e with
  | ANum n => [SPush n]
  | AId x => [SLoad x]
  | APlus a1 a2 => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SPlus]
  | AMinus a1 a2 => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SMinus]
  | AMult a1 a2 => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SMult]
  end.

Example s_compile1 :
  s_compile <{ X - (2 * Y) }>
  = [SLoad X; SPush 2; SLoad Y; SMult; SMinus].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
stack_compiler.explanation: s_execute processes instructions one at a time, manipulating the stack. s_compile recursively compiles aexps to stack instructions.

execute_app.solution:
Theorem execute_app : forall st p1 p2 stack,
  s_execute st stack (p1 ++ p2) = s_execute st (s_execute st stack p1) p2.
Proof.
  intros st p1. induction p1 as [| i p1' IH]; intros p2 stack.
  - simpl. reflexivity.
  - simpl. destruct i;
      try (apply IH);
      try (destruct stack as [| n1 [| n2 rest]]; apply IH).
Qed.
execute_app.explanation: By induction on p1, executing p1 ++ p2 is the same as executing p1 then p2 on the resulting stack.

stack_compiler_correct.solution:
Lemma s_compile_correct_aux : forall st e stack,
  s_execute st stack (s_compile e) = aeval st e :: stack.
Proof.
  intros st e. induction e; intros stack; simpl;
    try reflexivity;
    try (rewrite execute_app; rewrite IHe1;
         rewrite execute_app; rewrite IHe2;
         simpl; reflexivity).
Qed.

Theorem s_compile_correct : forall (st : state) (e : aexp),
  s_execute st [] (s_compile e) = [ aeval st e ].
Proof.
  intros st e. apply s_compile_correct_aux.
Qed.
stack_compiler_correct.explanation: The auxiliary lemma generalizes over the stack. For compound expressions, we use execute_app to decompose the compiled program.

break_imp.solution:
Inductive ceval : com -> state -> result -> state -> Prop :=
  | E_Skip : forall st,
      st =[ CSkip ]=> st / SContinue
  | E_Break : forall st,
      st =[ CBreak ]=> st / SBreak
  | E_Asgn : forall st a n x,
      aeval st a = n ->
      st =[ CAsgn x a ]=> (x !-> n ; st) / SContinue
  | E_SeqBreak : forall c1 c2 st st',
      st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SBreak ->
      st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st' / SBreak
  | E_SeqContinue : forall c1 c2 st st' st'' s,
      st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SContinue ->
      st' =[ c2 ]=> st'' / s ->
      st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st'' / s
  | E_IfTrue : forall st st' b c1 c2 s,
      beval st b = true ->
      st =[ c1 ]=> st' / s ->
      st =[ CIf b c1 c2 ]=> st' / s
  | E_IfFalse : forall st st' b c1 c2 s,
      beval st b = false ->
      st =[ c2 ]=> st' / s ->
      st =[ CIf b c1 c2 ]=> st' / s
  | E_WhileFalse : forall b st c,
      beval st b = false ->
      st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st / SContinue
  | E_WhileTrue : forall st st' st'' b c,
      beval st b = true ->
      st =[ c ]=> st' / SContinue ->
      st' =[ CWhile b c ]=> st'' / SContinue ->
      st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st'' / SContinue
  | E_WhileBreak : forall st st' b c,
      beval st b = true ->
      st =[ c ]=> st' / SBreak ->
      st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / SContinue
  where "st '=[' c ']=>' st' '/' s" := (ceval c st s st').

Theorem break_ignore : forall c st st' s,
     st =[ CSeq CBreak c ]=> st' / s ->
     st = st'.
Proof.
  intros c st st' s H. inversion H; subst.
  - inversion H5. subst. reflexivity.
  - inversion H2.
Qed.

Theorem while_continue : forall b c st st' s,
  st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / s ->
  s = SContinue.
Proof.
  intros b c st st' s H. inversion H; subst; reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem while_stops_on_break : forall b c st st',
  beval st b = true ->
  st =[ c ]=> st' / SBreak ->
  st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / SContinue.
Proof.
  intros b c st st' Hb Hc.
  apply E_WhileBreak; assumption.
Qed.

Theorem seq_continue : forall c1 c2 st st' st'',
  st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SContinue ->
  st' =[ c2 ]=> st'' / SContinue ->
  st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st'' / SContinue.
Proof.
  intros c1 c2 st st' st'' H1 H2.
  apply E_SeqContinue with st'; assumption.
Qed.

Theorem seq_stops_on_break : forall c1 c2 st st',
  st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SBreak ->
  st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st' / SBreak.
Proof.
  intros c1 c2 st st' H.
  apply E_SeqBreak. assumption.
Qed.
break_imp.explanation: Extend Imp with break by adding signal propagation. Break produces SBreak; sequences check signals; while handles break by terminating the loop.

while_break_true.solution:
Theorem while_break_true : forall b c st st',
  st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / SContinue ->
  beval st' b = true ->
  exists st'', st'' =[ c ]=> st' / SBreak.
Proof.
  intros b c st st' H Hb.
  remember (CWhile b c) as cw eqn:Heqcw.
  induction H; inversion Heqcw; subst.
  - rewrite Hb in H. discriminate.
  - apply IHceval2; try reflexivity; try assumption.
  - exists st. assumption.
Qed.
while_break_true.explanation: By induction on the while evaluation derivation: if the loop terminates normally with guard true, then some iteration must have broken.

ceval_deterministic.solution:
Theorem ceval_deterministic: forall (c:com) st st1 st2 s1 s2,
     st =[ c ]=> st1 / s1 ->
     st =[ c ]=> st2 / s2 ->
     st1 = st2 /\ s1 = s2.
Proof.
  intros c st st1 st2 s1 s2 E1 E2.
  generalize dependent s2. generalize dependent st2.
  induction E1; intros st2 s2 E2; inversion E2; subst;
    try (split; reflexivity);
    try discriminate.
  - (* SeqBreak vs SeqBreak *)
    apply IHE1 in H4. destruct H4. subst. split; reflexivity.
  - (* SeqBreak vs SeqContinue *)
    apply IHE1 in H1. destruct H1. discriminate.
  - (* SeqContinue vs SeqBreak *)
    apply IHE1_1 in H4. destruct H4. discriminate.
  - (* SeqContinue vs SeqContinue *)
    apply IHE1_1 in H1. destruct H1. subst.
    apply IHE1_2 in H5. destruct H5. split; assumption.
  - (* IfTrue vs IfTrue *)
    apply IHE1 in H6. assumption.
  - rewrite H in H5. discriminate.
  - rewrite H in H5. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H6. assumption.
  - rewrite H in H3. discriminate.
  - rewrite H in H1. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H4. destruct H4. subst.
    apply IHE1_2 in H7. assumption.
  - apply IHE1 in H5. destruct H5. discriminate.
  - rewrite H in H5. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H4. destruct H4. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H4. destruct H4. subst. split; reflexivity.
Qed.
ceval_deterministic.explanation: Determinism for break_imp: by induction on one derivation, inverting the other, using IH to match signals and states.

short_circuit.solution:
(* This is an optional exercise requiring new definitions inside a module. *)
(* Best-effort: define short-circuit beval and prove equivalence. *)
Fixpoint beval_sc (st : state) (b : bexp) : bool :=
  match b with
  | BTrue => true
  | BFalse => false
  | BEq a1 a2 => (aeval st a1) =? (aeval st a2)
  | BNeq a1 a2 => negb ((aeval st a1) =? (aeval st a2))
  | BLe a1 a2 => (aeval st a1) <=? (aeval st a2)
  | BGt a1 a2 => negb ((aeval st a1) <=? (aeval st a2))
  | BNot b1 => negb (beval_sc st b1)
  | BAnd b1 b2 => if beval_sc st b1 then beval_sc st b2 else false
  end.

Theorem beval_sc_equiv : forall st b,
  beval_sc st b = beval st b.
Proof.
  intros st b. induction b; simpl; try reflexivity;
    try (rewrite IHb; reflexivity);
    rewrite IHb1; rewrite IHb2; destruct (beval st b1); reflexivity.
Qed.
short_circuit.explanation: Short-circuit evaluation of BAnd checks b1 first: if false, returns false immediately. This is equivalent to normal beval since expressions have no side effects.

XtimesYinZ_spec.solution:
Theorem XtimesYinZ_spec : forall st n m st',
  st X = n ->
  st Y = m ->
  st =[ XtimesYinZ ]=> st' ->
  st' Z = n * m.
Proof.
  intros st n m st' HX HY Heval.
  inversion Heval. subst. simpl.
  apply t_update_eq.
Qed.
XtimesYinZ_spec.explanation: XtimesYinZ assigns Z := X * Y. After inversion, st' Z equals aeval st (X * Y) = st X * st Y.

add_for_loop.solution:
(* Design exercise: Add C-style for loops to Imp.

   Syntax:
     CFor (init : com) (guard : bexp) (update : com) (body : com)

   Semantics (as a derived form):
     for(init; guard; update) { body }
   is equivalent to:
     init; while guard do body; update end

   Add to com:
     | CFor (c_init : com) (b : bexp) (c_update : com) (c_body : com)

   Add evaluation rules:
     | E_For : forall st st' c_init b c_update c_body,
         st =[ c_init; while b do c_body; c_update end ]=> st' ->
         st =[ CFor c_init b c_update c_body ]=> st'
*)
add_for_loop.explanation: Design exercise: C-style for loops can be desugared to init; while guard do body; update end. Add CFor to com and E_For that delegates to the sequence+while translation.
